artifact.name: BD_01_CAPTURE_PROOF_INTEGRITY_REPORT.json
artifact.title: Repair capture proof integrity before using screenshots as acceptance evidence

prior_gate.effect: Item-level burndown truth and True-UX authority govern this run; stale screenshots and unproven bundles are reference-only until proof eligibility is present.

implemented: Capture run provenance in routes-and-modals index output.
Machine-readable screenshot proof policy in capture index output.
Capture QA warning when routes-and-modals manifests omit proof eligibility.
Contract tests for the new proof integrity boundary.

changed_files.change: Adds CaptureRunProvenance, CaptureProofPolicy, per-item proofEligibility and fixed captureViewport provenance.
Reads proofEligibility.status from routes-and-modals manifests and warns when missing.
Locks the new provenance and proof eligibility contract.
Updates the passing fixture to include proof eligibility.

reasons: Source state is dirty; screenshot cannot be treated as clean-baseline proof.
Screens-only mode has no DOM/CSS/runtime sidecars, so it is supporting visual evidence only.

proof_policy.acceptance_boundary: Screenshots are visual QA evidence only. They must not be promoted to COMPLETE_VERTICAL_SLICE proof without current-run UI, handler/service, DB/state, guard/audit and test evidence.

overclaim_control.before: Capture manifests had rich metadata, but screenshots could still be read too generously as acceptance proof.
overclaim_control.after: Every new routes-and-modals capture declares run provenance, proof policy and per-item eligibility. Dirty/source-incomplete/screens-only captures are explicitly downgraded.
overclaim_control.remaining_limit: A visual_acceptance_candidate is still not COMPLETE_VERTICAL_SLICE proof. Process capability still needs UI, handler/service, DB/state, guard/audit and test evidence.

next_ticket.title: Current-user, tenant, role and object-scope foundation
next_ticket.note: This is a high-CTES foundation/spec ticket and should be executed as subtasks BD-02-A, BD-02-B and BD-02-C, one at a time.
